title: Everything's Better on a Stick!
story: Corn dogs, popsicles, cotton candy, and kebabs all have something in common – they're stuck on sticks! National Something on a Stick Day celebrates every March 28, and people go wild finding creative foods to skewer. Families have stick-food parties where everything from marshmallows to cheese cubes gets the stick treatment. Restaurants join in by offering special stick menus, and social media fills with photos of the weirdest stick creations people can imagine. Some folks even put cake pops, fruit kabobs, and mini sandwiches on sticks just for fun. It's amazing how putting food on a stick makes it taste better and way more exciting to eat!
activity_question: What do people often do on National Something on a Stick Day?
activity_choices: Have stick-food parties with friends and family
activity_answer: Have stick-food parties with friends and family
follow_up_question: What's the weirdest food you think would taste better on a stick?

title: Sweet Layers of Black Forest Bliss
story: Chocolate cake lovers unite every March 28 for National Black Forest Cake Day! This amazing German dessert features layers of rich chocolate cake, fluffy whipped cream, and juicy cherries. People celebrate by baking their own Black Forest cakes or visiting bakeries to buy a slice. Many families make it a tradition to share this special treat together. The cake gets its name from the Black Forest region in Germany, where it was first created. Today, bakers around the world put their own spin on this classic dessert!
activity_question: What are the main ingredients that make up a Black Forest cake?
activity_choices: Chocolate cake, whipped cream, and cherries
Vanilla cake, chocolate frosting, and strawberries
Carrot cake, cream cheese, and walnuts
Banana cake, peanut butter, and marshmallows
activity_answer: Chocolate cake, whipped cream, and cherries
bonus_fact: Traditional Black Forest cake is often topped with chocolate shavings and a cherry on top!

title: Purr-fect Day to Spoil Your Feline Friend
story: Cats everywhere get the royal treatment they deserve on March 28 during Respect Your Cat Day! Pet owners shower their furry friends with extra treats, new toys, and endless belly rubs. Some cats get fancy gourmet meals or cozy new beds to nap in. Social media fills with adorable cat photos and videos as people celebrate their pets' quirky personalities. Many cat cafes offer special events where visitors can play with adoptable cats. It's the perfect excuse to give your cat that expensive catnip toy they've been eyeing!
activity_question: What do many cat cafes offer on Respect Your Cat Day?
follow_up_question: What special way would you pamper your cat or a friend's cat on this day?

title: Apple-solutely Amazing Teachers' Day
story: Students in Czech Republic and Slovakia celebrate their teachers with special surprises on March 28! Kids bring flowers, small gifts, and handmade cards to show appreciation for their favorite educators. Many schools organize fun performances where students sing songs or put on plays for their teachers. Some classes even switch roles for part of the day, letting students try teaching while teachers become pupils. It's a wonderful way to say "thank you" to the people who help kids learn and grow every single day.
activity_question: What do students in Czech Republic and Slovakia often bring their teachers on this special day?
follow_up_question: What creative way would you show appreciation for your favorite teacher?
